column2: Future Bus Systems - Adiona
column4:
The purpose of the Future Bus Systems (FBS) contract (Contract Ref  ICT11898) is to provide Transport for London (TfL)  with a new system (known as Adiona), which will replace the functionality of three legacy systems that are at their ‘end of life’ (some over 20 years old) and have constraints that no longer met TfL’s changing needs. The three systems are  
1. BusNet – contains bus route and schedule data;
2. Caesar – manages all the bus contracting data for all routes in London; and             
3.  SSTT (Stop Specific Timetables) – the system that creates all bus stop timetable information.

column2: OSO - LRS Refuse Collection
column4:
London River Services (LRS) owns eight piers (Greenwich, Tower, Bankside, Blackfriars, Festival, Embankment, Westminster and Millbank Pier) along the River Thames and is responsible for the maintenance, including refuse collection.

Currently, general refuse is collected from all eight piers 3 times per week. All piers have large wheelie bins where rubbish sacks are placed by the pier cleaning contractors.   The loose bags are then transferred to a storage barge and transported to a waste recycling facility in Barking. In addition to the general collections, ad-hoc collections are required if items are fly tipped onto TfL piers which does occasionally happen. If the boats who fly tip rubbish can be identified, the bill for the removal costs will be sent to them however this is not always possible.

Refuse from piers are collected by boat as most piers have very limited roadside access and navigating a waste collection vehicle near to a pier is difficult. Due to the tidal nature of the

column2: 2021-071 - Track Access Points Phase 2 Main Works
column4:
The lack of suitable Road-Rail access points around the DLR network present problems for Permanent Way related Capital projects. The most significant constraint being the difficulty in getting plants and equipment trackside to allow works to be undertaken during engineering hours and possessions. Trackside access has been relatively easy in the past due to the various vantage points around the network. However, with the lucrative nature of land and financial gain to TfL, most of the track access points have been lost to developers who plan to build close to the railway. Consequently, there is a vital need to secure and develop access points along the network to help with future Capital projects, planning and maintenance works.

This project will require the installation of new Road Rail Access Points and compounds including new rail infill panels, conductor rail gap, cable management system, concrete apron, hard standing, drainage and fencing.

column2: Debt Management and Enforcement Services - Bristow and Sutor
column4:
The proposed contract is for Debt Management and Enforcement Services within the UK.  TfL operates and enforces a number of Road User Charging (RUC) and Traffic Enforcement schemes throughout Greater London. These together contribute to the realisation of the Mayor’s Transport Strategy. 

This contract will primarily support the debt owed to TfL due to non compliance with RUC schemes and Traffic Enforcement (TE) schemes comprising the following 

• Congestion Charging
• Low Emission Zone (LEZ)
• Traffic Enforcement and
• Ultra Low Emission Zone (ULEZ) from April 2019 and
• ULEZ Extension 
• Direct Vision Standard (subject to consultation)

column2: TfL 94668 Administration of TfL’s Walking and Cycling Grants London Programme
column4:
As part of the Mayor’s Active Travel initiative, TfL are seeking a grants administrator to manage TfL’s Walking and Cycling grants programme to encourage walking and cycling amongst under represented audiences across 33 London boroughs. The grants administrator will need to be well versed and experienced in active travel, namely walking and cycling.  Grants will be awarded over a 3 year period, on a local (borough) basis to community projects, not for profit organisations and charitable organisations. The programme contributes towards the target of 80  sustainable mode of transport share by 2041 and improves health and reduces inactivity to help underrepresented group achieve their recommended 2 x 10 minutes of activity per day

The grants administrator will manage -

• Programme delivery from marketing and generating interest for applications to administering grants, providing support and reporting on programme delivery
• Marketing   PR for the programme
• Training and support needs

column2: PLU INF DC Regen Design
column4:
The 24TS has a different power requirement to the current rolling stock fleet which means elements of the existing electricity supply will need to be re-enforced with new 1000v resilient and compliant equipment. 

This procurement is for the design of the Direct Current (DC) Electrical Trackside Equipment (ETE) design for the Piccadilly Line.  The scope includes the following:

The Current On-Line Relays requirement is as follows.:
• Survey all 27No. COLRs, 
• Site the COLRs with TfL Signalling Works Delivery
• Produce Track Clearance Applications (if COLR is sited within 3m of the nearest running rail).
• Produce Space Applications per site.

3) Assessment of the media agency’s delivery against marketing objectives and KPIs, and their buying performance versus their contractual guarantees. 
4) Evaluation of the inventory received as a result of TfL’s direct deals with Global, JC Decaux and Metro – Ebiquity calculate the value of the inventory delivered and check that all guarantees have been met. 
5) Assessment of TfL’s digital advertising to ensure it meets agreed targets for Viewability, Fraud Prevention and Brand Safety.

column2: TfL95932 Lot 1 Task 07 Sporting Celebrations
column4:
There is increased public desire to celebrate, with English teams, on their return from victory at a tournament.  

Most notably, should the England men’s football team be successful at the EUROs in Germany this summer, London will host the victory parade in partnership with The FA and the DCMS.

The GLA is seeking to appoint an event production company by spring 2024, to ensure planning can commence with all London partners by early May, ahead of EURO 2024 kicking off in Friday 14 June. 

The approved budget of Ł1.5m has been ringfenced for sporting celebrations and is intended to be used in full for the UEFA Men’s EURO 2024 celebration in the event the national team is successful. 

If the entire funding amount is not spent on a men’s parade in 2024, the remainder of the funding will be used to deliver celebratory parades and/or screenings for future events over the next three years. MD3237 has ringfenced this unused funding to ensure that celebratory parades and screenings are easie

  This contract will primarily support the debt owed to TfL due to non compliance with RUC schemes and Traffic Enforcement (TE) schemes comprising the following 

• Congestion Charging
• Low Emission Zone (LEZ)
• Traffic Enforcement and
• Ultra Low Emission Zone (ULEZ) from April 2019 and
• ULEZ Extension 
• Direct Vision Standard (subject to consultation)

No cost to TfL - Revenue Generating.

column2: Great Eastern Stns Roof Remedials and Safe Access
column4:
RfLI is the landlord of the Great Eastern stations following a 125-year lease being granted from Network Rail in 2015.  

The condition in which these stations have been identified by RfLI, to be in such poor condition that replacement and/or repairs to the roofs are required. 
 
The exact scale of the issues that need to be resolved to bring these roofs to an acceptable standard has been identified in a suite of recent surveys conducted by a RfLI supplier. The survey works captured and catalogued a list of remedial works required to bring Great Eastern station roofing to a watertight, compliant, maintainable set of assets.  

With the majority of Great Eastern station roofs not supporting safe access the condition has been deteriorating at an accelerated rate and maintenance of gutters has not been possible causing issues particularly in Autumn. Works to enable safe access will need to be undertaken prior to the remedials works commencing to ensure achievable maintenance access at eac

column2: OSO - Thames Skills Academy Agreement
column4:
TfL/London River Services (LRS) are Board members of a non-for-profit organisation called Thames Skills Academy (TSA). This was set up in 2016, initiated through Tideway investment programme to help raise the safety standards and modernise the industry culture on the Thames. TfL, PLA and a number of organisations are on  the Board.
The TSA raises safety standards and improves skills for those working on and alongside the Thames by:

• Developing and arranging delivery of apprenticeships and training to meet the skills needs of regulators, employers, and employees
• Attracting and retaining a wider diversity of talent for the growing and diverse needs of the Thames.

The strategic plans for the TSA are detailed in appendix one (1) 'TSA strategy paper for TfL,' this sets out the TSA’s background, goals, key achievements.

column2: Network Rail Connection Charges 2019-26
column4: provision of Network Rail Connection Agreements for New Cross Gate, Old Kent Road and Dalston for part of FY 2020 21. The three rail connection points require permanent contractual agreements which not only secure LOs access rights from RFL managed infrastructure to that of Network rail, but also to ensure asset maintenance at those connection points can be appropriately allocated (rails, joints, signals, points etc.) There are agreed ongoing charges for these agreements based upon asset maintenance cost. As London Overground operates on NR infrastructure, connection agreements are required at the three connection points; New Cross Gate (for services south to West Croydon   Crystal Palace), Old Kent Road Juction (for services west to Clapham Junction on the South London Lines) and Dalston Junction (for services north to Highbury   Islington). These connection agreements allow our RFL ELL Core Route between Dalston Junction and Surrey Quays to seamlessly connect to the NR infrastructure

Following the completion of this package the roof should be water-tight and the MEP equipment within the building should be protected.

column2: TfL 96902 – Commercial Manager for RUC Re-let Programme
column4:
The TfL’s Procurement & Supply Chain, Capital delivery team require a Commercial Manager to work within a team delivering business critical activities across London’s Road User Charging (RUC) infrastructure portfolio. 

 RUC is TfL’s strategic programme for delivering air quality improvements across London, and delivers the Congestion Charge, Low Emission, Ultra Low Emission Zone and Tunnel User Charging schemes. The RUC infrastructure, system and services are among the most high profile of their type in the world and are a reference site for other global cities looking to manage their air quality and traffic congestion.  

This is a critical time for the dynamic RUC Relet delivery team, with planning for the re-procurement of the three major underpinning contracts in 2026 already underway, and a host of other live change projects and workstreams in process which define the future landscape for strategic management of London’s charging and air quality infrastructure.

column2: ICT14202 OT DETECTION TOOL
Postal Services for the GLA
column4:
Postal Services at the GLA - Crystal Building.

This services is for managing the GLA's outsourced postal service, covering both inbound and outbound mail. The service is predominantly a digital service which involves the electronic scanning of inbound post and onward distribution to recipients by electronic means, although certain cherished items such as cheques, VIP invitations and some legal documents are sent to recipients in their original form. 

Outbound mail is predominantly uploaded to the mail system where it is then printed, put into an envelop with postage paid and dispatched into the Royal Mail system. In addition, all inbound post is security screened for physical threats, such as sharp items and IEDs, any threats that are identified are reported to the police by the supplier and the GLA informed.

column2: S Stock Supply and Repair of S-Stock CAB Handsets
column4:
This Paper is a interim basis Single Source contract for 3 years to DAC Limited to supply parts for the repair of the S-Stock handsets and new handsets if deemed unrepairable. This supports the Fleet Maintenance programme of work.
The cab handset is situated within the train operators seating area and is used for contacting other staff (e.g. controller, signaller and the cab at the other end of the train), making public addresses and contacting passengers after a passenger emergency alarm. As stated in S2180 (the standard for passenger rolling stock) this item is safety critical and a train cannot be put into service with a known defective handset. 
The scope of components purchased from DAC consists of a stowage repair kit that can be used by Fleet Maintenance and where this is not possible the items are sent to the supplier (DAC Ltd) for repair. It also includes purchase of replacement handsets in the unlikely event that these may be required.

column2: 96793 TfL - Student Housing Consultancy
column4:
Places for London has clear long-term objectives to deliver homes for Londoners and deliver long-term sustainable income.  

Places are looking at the opportunity to deliver Student Housing in London. This will enhance Places for London’s residential investments to meet Places’ strategic goals; delivery of long-term income and home delivery. Places has a pipeline of homes and by the end of the Business Plan, Places wants to significantly increase its holdings of residential assets as part of it’s portfolio mix. This will grow Places asset value.  

Places for London has limited experience delivering and transacting in the student sector. The sector is a specialised sub-market of the Living sector and constitutes housing delivery under the London Plan (2.5 bedrooms/units are counted as a single home), supporting Places’ objective of delivering 20,000 homes.

column2: ICT14480 Virtu Rail Annual Support
column4:
VirtuRail is a piece of software that allows an interactive, engaging view into the virtual reality created by TfL’s Railway Engineering Simulator (RES).   
The Builder allows the construction of a 3D world based on RES input data. Geographical data errors can be identified and fixed.  The environment can be customised to show various types of tunnel and urban, suburban linesides. Then various types of trains are added. When RES outputs are fed in, trains and signals animate to visualise railway events from a multitude of viewpoints.

column2: B S Assessments - Access Support FY23 24 plus
column4: The Civils Bridges & Structures Assessment team of London Underground (LU) Engineering are responsible for carrying out structural assessments of Bridges and Structures assets on the TFL estate. Structural Assessments, which are safety-critical in most instances, are carried out at the request of civils projects and civils maintenance department across various business units of TFL. Every structural assessment requires an inspection for assessment as per LU standard S1061. An inspection for assessment requires preparation of method statements, risk assessments (RAMS), organising access and providing protection, labour and the equipment needed. Support services are required as a “one-stop-shop” for providing all these requirements, to mitigate risks and delays to the project.

These logs are then used to generate alerts for investigation by the Cyber Security Operations Centre helping to protect TfL from the risk of a cyber-attack. column2: (CW) Walking and Cycling Grants London (Grant fund)
column4:
As part of the Mayor’s Active Travel initiative, TfL are seeking a grants administrator to manage TfL’s Walking and Cycling grants programme to encourage walking and cycling amongst under represented audiences across 33 London boroughs. The grants administrator will need to be well versed and experienced in active travel, namely walking and cycling.  Grants will be awarded over a 3 year period, on a local (borough) basis to community projects, not for profit organisations and charitable organisations. The programme contributes towards the target of 80  sustainable mode of transport share by 2041 and improves health and reduces inactivity to help underrepresented group achieve their recommended 2 x 10 minutes of activity per day

The grants administrator will manage -

• Programme delivery from marketing and generating interest for applications to administering grants, providing support and reporting on programme delivery
• Marketing   PR for the programme
• Training and support needs

column2: Greenwich Power Station Fire Suppression
column4:
The current fire suppression system has to be manually operated to activate the sprinkler and fire curtain systems to supress an oil fire.  Delay in operation could result in further damage and/or fire spreading to other part of the power station building

The project intends to replace the current fire alarm panels and control valves linked to the fire suppression systems, integrating new fire alarm control panels and upgrading the existing fire suppression systems at Greenwich Power Station

The following is required to supply, install and commissioned: 

- Modify/replace valves/actuators to enable automatic operation. 
- New fire control panel to automate the sequence and interface with the existing site fire detection system. 
- Isolators and control relays (I/O) for operation of suppression system. 
- Cabling work. 
- Drawings and test certificates

column2: Heathrow Support in Install ULEZ Cameras
column4:
As part of the London-wide ULEZ 2023 TfL will need to install 6 cameras at Heathrow Airport. Heathrow owns and maintains certain roads within the area of the Heathrow Airport and according to the Aerodrome Traffic (Heathrow) Order 1976 (SI 1976 No. 1495) is conferred with functions that are normally exercisable by the highway authority or local authority in relation to those roads. 

In August 2023 TfL and Heathrow signed a consent Agreement which allows TfL to install traffic signs and boundary cameras in support of the London-wide ULEZ on roads owned by Heathrow. There are 6 ANPR boundary cameras to be installed by Yunex on Heathrow land. However, due to safety and security restrictions and controls in place, Heathrow only permit their own appointed contractors or contractors that have completed their training to access and connect to the power supply networks on their land.
